title: Privacy: A0 fixtures from ~/.claude/projects can leak prompts and paths
body: A0 asks for real ~/.claude/projects JSONL excerpts under tests/fixtures/persona-attribution/. Those can include private prompts, file paths, tool inputs, and message text. Gitignore only covers generated dashboard JSONL, not committed fixtures. Require minimized, redacted fixtures with only linkage, IDs, timestamps, model, and usage fields.

title: A6 'top-3 per gate' undefined when fewer than 3 personas qualify
body: What does /wrap-insights do for a gate with only 1 or 2 personas with runs_in_window ≥ 3? Show all available, omit the section, show with a '(only N qualifying)' note? Lock the behavior so a test can assert it.

title: Persona-author exposure without action — leaderboard pressure even without auto-pruning
body: A sortable public-by-default dashboard column showing 'scope-discipline: 32% survival, 14% uniqueness' reads as a leaderboard whether intended or not. Even with no auto-pruning, this creates social pressure on persona authors to 'improve their numbers' rather than improve review quality.

title: A4 content-hash test has hidden timing dependency
body: 'Run /wrap-insights twice' assumes the second run actually picks up at least one new (persona, gate) invocation between runs — otherwise runs_in_window would be 0, not 1. State the precondition: 'after editing the persona file, run one fresh gate dispatch that uses that persona, THEN run /wrap-insights.'

title: e1 'new persona, no data' rows impossible under data-driven artifact model
body: Artifact: 'one row per (persona, gate) pair seen'. e1 requires rows for new personas with no data. Those personas are not 'seen' until they run. Decide: roster-driven (script reads current persona files + emits empty row regardless of seen-ness) or data-driven (no row for unseen personas; e1 only kicks in once persona has ≥1 run). Lean: data-driven (smaller scope).

title: No outcome / success criterion for the spec itself — A0–A9 are all internal
body: Round 1's stakeholder reviewer flagged this; the rewrite removed the auto-pruning use case but didn't add an instrumentation-success metric. Add: 'after first /wrap-insights run on a project with ≥10 historical gate runs, the JSONL contains ≥1 row per (persona, gate) pair seen in those runs.' Without this it is possible to ship A0–A9 green with an empty JSONL.

title: 'Merge target' is not defined in the spec
body: Survival semantics hinge on whether a finding is 'the merge target,' but the spec never defines what makes one finding the target vs the merged-in. Presumably a Judge-stage concept, but a reader of this spec cannot determine it. Moot if Blocker #1 fixed.

title: /wrap-insights rendering surface (27 datapoints) will demand deltas/week-over-week
body: Day-after ask will be deltas/week-over-week. Add Open Question stating Phase 1c stays static in v1; deltas defer to BACKLOG.

title: Phase 0 spike-failure path triggers third spec revision, not in-flight expansion
body: Spec says 'if no clean linkage, stop and consider a logging shim.' But the logging-shim path means modifying every Agent dispatch site across all gate commands — that's a separate scope expansion. State explicitly that the logging-shim path triggers a third spec revision, not in-flight expansion.

title: /wrap-insights refresh path overstated — dashboard-append.sh is conditional on graphify
body: Spec assumes /wrap-insights runs dashboard-append.sh and therefore recomputes rankings. In current wrap.md, dashboard append is conditional on graphify-out/graph.json existing, so many projects skip it entirely. Make persona-ranking refresh its own unconditional Phase 1c step.

title: Jaccard scope ambiguity — 'same gate run' undefined
body: Moot if Blocker #2 fixed (jaccard removed). Otherwise: state that uniqueness is computed within run_id (or whatever the canonical key is in findings.jsonl), and add 'uniqueness computed per gate-run, not all-pairs across window'.

title: BACKLOG #3 trigger condition '≥10 runs of validated data' is soft
body: 'Validated' undefined. Tie validation to A1 + A3 passing, making the trigger mechanical.

title: Survival semantics: 'body includes content from this persona's raw output' undefined
body: Spec says 'a finding survives if the post-Judge findings.jsonl contains a row whose personas[] includes this persona AND whose body includes content from this persona's raw output.' 'Includes content from' has no operational definition — substring match? token overlap? jaccard again? Moot if Blocker #1 fixed (pure personas[] membership rule). Flag for /plan not to reintroduce body-matching.

title: A1 ±5% token-sum invariant is structurally impossible — reframe before /plan
body: Probe (73 Agent dispatches) confirmed: subagent assistant turns are NOT in the parent session JSONL; orchestrator dominates session totals (1203/1203 assistant rows in fixture). Per-persona sum cannot equal session total ±5%. Fix: rewrite A1 as sum(per_persona_tokens) == sum(known_child_Agent_usage_rows) (exact equality), with separate diagnostic columns orchestrator_tokens and unattributed_tokens reported but not constrained.

title: Uniqueness should use existing unique_to_persona field, not re-jaccard cluster titles
body: Current findings.jsonl rows are already clusters and already carry personas[] plus unique_to_persona. Re-running jaccard on synthesized titles measures title similarity between clusters, not whether multiple personas surfaced the same finding. It will create false duplicates and false uniques. Fix: uniqueness rate = count(rows where unique_to_persona == persona) / count(rows where persona ∈ personas[]). Drop jaccard entirely.

title: Cross-project aggregation assumes persona identity is global — but hashes are local
body: Spec says persona value is a property of the persona, not the project. That breaks if two checkouts have different persona files, command prompts, or workflow versions. Existing findings.jsonl rows do not record persona content hash, so the proposed content-hash reset cannot be applied to old or remote-project runs.

title: Drill-down from aggregate row to source findings missing
body: When a persona shows surprising numbers, there's no --explain mode, no contributing_run_ids[]/contributing_finding_ids[] field, no link from the aggregate to the underlying findings. persona-metrics-validator validates schema, not 'show me the rows.'

title: Insufficient-sample rows only opacity-dimmed — buried in styling
body: A persona with runs_in_window < 3 renders with reduced opacity but the row is still written and survival_rate / uniqueness_rate still appear as numbers. A reader sorting the table by survival_rate will see 'new-persona: 100% survival (1 run)' at the top — technically dimmed, but still ranked #1. Either hide insufficient-sample rows from default sort or replace the rate cell with '—'.

title: Concurrency: last-writer-wins is unbounded — stale long-running compute can clobber
body: A stale long-running compute can clobber a fresh fast one. Either freshness-check before replace, or document acceptable staleness.

title: Single-consumer dashboard tab justification — defer to v1.1?
body: Justin is the only human reading dashboard/data/persona-rankings.jsonl until #3 ships. A new sortable tab with color-banded tertiles is non-trivial UX work for an audience of one. The /wrap-insights text sub-section likely covers the actual decision-support need at 5% of the dashboard cost.

title: Within-run multi-dispatch counting unit — one Agent tool_use_id = one invocation
body: Counting unit needs to handle re-prompts within a single gate run. Lock: one Agent tool_use_id = one invocation, regardless of re-prompts.

title: No diagnostic CLI surface — --dry-run, --explain, --list-projects, --project missing
body: compute-persona-value.py needs operator-facing diagnostics. Add to A7 test coverage.

title: Persona-name regex caveats — autorun, kickoff agents, description-invoked subagents
body: Regex confirmed working across 73 fixtures, but: /autorun headless flow may scaffold prompts differently (validate against an autorun fixture); project-specific kickoff agents are excluded (document); description-invoked subagents (persona-metrics-validator) should map to persona: <unknown>, not silently drop.

title: Cross-project root discovery unspecified, but A3 already depends on it
body: Open Q4 punts to /plan, yet A3 requires that /wrap-insights surfaces data from another MonsterFlow project. Cost lives under ~/.claude/projects/*/, value under ~/Projects/*/docs/specs/*/findings.jsonl — distinct trees. Need a MonsterFlow-project predicate (sentinel file, presence of commands/spec-review.md, or explicit config). ~/Projects/* contains non-MonsterFlow trees.

title: Survival is not computable from current artifacts — use personas[] membership
body: Spec defines survival as retained/chosen by Judge in findings.jsonl, including 'merge target' semantics. But current findings.jsonl is already the synthesized post-cluster output; it does not record pre-Judge rows, merge target, merged-but-not-target contributors, or raw source spans. Existing survival.jsonl means 'addressed/included in next artifact', not 'survived Judge'. Fix: redefine survival as count(findings.jsonl rows where persona ∈ personas[]) / count(bullets in raw/<persona>.md).

title: Cost↔value join undefined — drop per-invocation averaging or add run_id bridge
body: Spec windows over 'last 45 (persona, gate) invocations' but cost (Claude session JSONL) and value (findings.jsonl) live in different artifact families with no shared (project, feature, gate, run_id) key. If average cost is over N invocations and average value is over M invocations and N≠M, ratios are meaningless. Fix: report total tokens + total emitted + total survived + total unique across the window; let dashboard divide.

title: A0/A1/A3/A8 testability gaps — honor-system, manual fixtures, unbounded carve-outs
body: A0 has no machine check ('spec contains a block' is honor-system). A1 cost-conservation undefined for 'no clean linkage' branch. A3 cross-project test depends on manually-created fixtures (not repeatable). A8 idempotency carries unbounded 'excluding last_seen' carve-out.

title: Open Q4 + Q5 already resolved in body — remove from Open Questions
body: Open Q4 (cross-project root) is resolved in Approach narrative. Open Q5 (deletion of stale rows) is resolved in e7 ('until the window rolls out'). Remove from Open Questions to avoid /plan re-litigating.

title: Pro-friend stakeholder explicitly orphaned in v1 — needs spec acknowledgment
body: The friend on Pro who diagnosed the original cost problem is the named stakeholder in BACKLOG #1, yet this spec ships them zero relief — their roster still dispatches all 28 personas at the same cost. Spec should state: 'this delivers no cost reduction to Pro users in v1; relief depends on BACKLOG #3 landing immediately after.' User has committed to v1.1 fast-follow.
